description: Machine-readable map from ModelBuilder v8 APIs to their v9 equivalents. Intended for AI agents and tooling performing an automated upgrade. The human-readable source of truth is MIGRATION.md; this file encodes its tables as structured data.

paradigm.summary: v9 replaces v8's runtime reflection build pipeline with compile-time source-generated builders. There is no back-compat shim; every removed v8 type is a throwing, [Obsolete(error: true)] stub so usage fails at build time.
paradigm.implication: Migration is compile-error driven: bump the package, build, and the compiler emits one error per v8 usage. Fix each using the mappings below, rebuild, and run the tests.

verification.successCriteria: Solution builds with no errors (v9 fails unbuildable usage at compile time, not runtime) and all tests pass.

mappings.notes: Create<T>() no longer accepts constructor arguments and always generates them. Construct<T>().From(...) emits a typed From overload per public constructor.
One Create method; no IsMatch / Generate(Type, ...). Matching moves to registration (AddValueSource<T>(source, "MemberName")).
Create calls the constructor and returns the instance; the engine populates it afterwards.
The T fixes the type; the member name is declared at registration.
Same registration shape; Create calls the constructor.
MinCount / MaxCount / NullPercentage / MaxDepth on BuildOptions. Per-member overrides (e.g. an age range) move to a custom IValueSource<T>.
Targeted form unchanged; type-agnostic IgnoreAny is new.
The fast-value shortcut becomes an ordinary named source.
The built-in sources register automatically; a custom module only adds deltas.
Typed, thread-safe, seedable, no boxing, no precision loss.

removed.guidance: No equivalent. Move to the supported seams: IValueSource<T>, mappings, ignore rules, [GenerateModelBuilder]. Construction, member discovery and ordering are decided at compile time, so there is no runtime pipeline to extend.
No equivalent. Construction and member resolution are performed by the source generator.
No equivalent. Caching is handled internally by the source generator.

removed.v8: Type / PropertyInfo / ParameterInfo generator overload triplets
removed.guidance: No equivalent. Per-ParameterInfo/PropertyInfo overloads are gone; member handling is compile-time.
Abstract and interface targets need an explicit Mapping<,>; there is no assembly scan that guesses a concrete type.

diagnostics.title: Abstract or interface build root has no mapping
diagnostics.fix: Add Model.Mapping<TAbstract, TConcrete>() or build a concrete type instead.

diagnostics.title: Build root has no accessible constructor
diagnostics.fix: Add a public constructor or expose an accessible one.

diagnostics.title: Model.Create(typeof(X)) names a type that cannot be built
diagnostics.fix: Call Model.Create<X>() somewhere, add a Mapping<,>, or annotate the type with [GenerateModelBuilder].

diagnostics.title: Use of a removed v8 API (an [Obsolete(error)] shim)
diagnostics.fix: Apply the matching entry from 'mappings' or 'removed'. The obsolete message names the v9 replacement.

unsupported: No runtime reflection / no dynamic or runtime-only types: a Type known only at runtime cannot be built (no reflection fallback).
Only compile-time-discoverable types are buildable (reachable from a call site, a mapping, or [GenerateModelBuilder]).
Visibility limits: private/protected types, constructors and members cannot be built; internal only via [InternalsVisibleTo] to the test assembly.
